presentation_context.teaching_goal: refresh the lecture, verify dated claims and improve logical flow

topics_covered.name: Bayesian computational cost and posterior sampling methods
topics_covered.coverage_note: Strong recent OA coverage for cost drivers and MCMC alternatives; didactic likelihood-before-posterior material is thinner.

topics_covered.name: Variational inference: variance properties and motivation relative to MCMC
topics_covered.coverage_note: Good evidence on variance behaviour and cost-vs-accuracy trade-off; conditions for variance underestimation well documented.

suggested_updates.finding: Recent work shows the blanket statement that Bayesian inference is computationally expensive at scale is dated: scalable and approximate posterior methods (Hamiltonian Monte Carlo, sequential and nested sampling, neural posterior estimation) bring large-scale inference into a practical regime, with cost driven by model dimension and posterior geometry rather than the Bayesian framing itself.
suggested_updates.rationale_vs_presentation: CLM_001 is currently presented as a flat limitation. The lecture should qualify it: cost depends on method and problem geometry, and modern samplers materially reduce it.

evidence_refs.quote: Mixing and per-iteration cost in MCMC for high-dimensional models are dominated by curvature of the posterior, not by the Bayesian formulation.
Amortized neural posterior estimation yields posterior approximations at near-constant inference cost after training.

suggested_updates.finding: MCMC is not the exclusive route to the posterior. Nested sampling, sequential Monte Carlo and simulation-based inference are now established alternatives, each with distinct cost and accuracy profiles, and are used where MCMC mixes poorly.
suggested_updates.rationale_vs_presentation: CLM_003 frames MCMC as the standard or sole sampler. The lecture should position MCMC as one option among several and name the alternatives.

evidence_refs.quote: Nested sampling provides both posterior samples and the marginal likelihood, complementing MCMC where evidence estimation is needed.
Synthetic-likelihood and simulation-based inference target settings with intractable likelihoods that MCMC cannot address directly.

suggested_updates.finding: The claim that variational inference always underestimates posterior variance holds for classic mean-field families but is not universal: richer families such as normalizing flows and structured/full-rank approximations can recover variance, so the failure mode is conditional on the chosen family.
suggested_updates.rationale_vs_presentation: CLM_002 is stated as an absolute. The lecture should bound it to mean-field VI and note that expressive families mitigate it.

evidence_refs.quote: Mean-field variational inference systematically underestimates marginal variances; the bias shrinks substantially with structured or flow-based posteriors.

optional_improvements.finding: A clean master-level exposition that introduces the likelihood function first and derives the posterior from it via Bayes' theorem, suitable as a bridge slide before the posterior is used.
optional_improvements.rationale_vs_presentation: F_001 flags that the posterior (C1) is used on slides 6-7 before the likelihood is explained. This source supplies a ready ordering to add a bridge.

evidence_refs.quote: We present the likelihood as the data-generating model and obtain the posterior as its normalized product with the prior.

optional_improvements.finding: A cost-versus-accuracy comparison motivating variational inference as a response to MCMC cost: VI trades exactness for speed and scales to large models, which is a natural narrative hook after MCMC limitations are introduced.
optional_improvements.rationale_vs_presentation: Gives the lecture a motivated transition from MCMC cost (CLM_001) to VI (C3), addressing the flow concern F_002.

evidence_refs.quote: Variational methods reduce wall-clock inference time by one to two orders of magnitude relative to full MCMC at a controlled accuracy cost.

unresolved_items.question: Does the cost advantage of neural/simulation-based inference hold for the small-data master-course examples, or only at large scale?
unresolved_items.why_unresolved: Reviewed open-access sources benchmark on large or simulator-based problems; none cover the lecture's small teaching examples.
unresolved_items.what_would_resolve: A source benchmarking amortized inference on low-dimensional, small-sample didactic problems.

source_refs.title: Challenges in Markov Chain Monte Carlo for Bayesian Neural Networks
Neural posterior estimation for astrophysical inference
Bayesian learning for neural networks: an algorithmic survey
Putting variation into variance: variational families and posterior variance

limitations: Open-access only; closed-access version-of-record papers were captured as metadata stubs and not read.
A08 claim verification was skipped in scout_fast mode; relations are reviewer-level, not formal claim verdicts.
Per-paper review used bounded targeted windows under the topic lens, not full-text reads.
